Like all aminoglycosides, it carries risk of ototoxicity (hearing loss) and nephrotoxicity.\n\n**MT-RNR1**: This is the mitochondrial 12S ribosomal RNA gene. Variants in this gene, particularly m.1555A>G and m.1494C>T, are well-known to cause hypersensitivity to aminoglycoside-induced ototoxicity. These variants make the mitochondrial ribosome more susceptible to aminoglycoside binding, which interferes with mitochondrial protein synthesis in cochlear hair cells.\n \nWith an uncertain risk variant in MT-RNR1, the patient falls into a gray zone—the variant isn't a known pathogenic one like m.1555A>G, but it's also not clearly benign. Given that aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss is irreversible and potentially severe, I should recommend a cautious approach even with uncertain variants.\n\nThe practical strategy would be to first explore non-aminoglycoside alternatives if clinically viable, but if netilmicin is truly necessary, use it with enhanced audiological monitoring rather than an absolute contraindication. This uncertainty likely warrants a \"Moderate\" risk classification—more cautious than no variant, but less restrictive than known pathogenic variants. -induced sensorineural hearing loss through altered mitochondrial ribosomal function, though the exact risk profile remains unclear, so I need to recommend cautious use with close monitoring.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1186,"cache_read_input_tokens":18420,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1186,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":0,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6fa84c80-c16b-44bb-8dbe-b015930c60ee","uuid":"abf3c201-1708-480e-a87f-04d796219bee"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0143DmNyxwHBkogWfDrJz42y","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The file is written and contains valid JSON. Here is a summary of the clinical reasoning:\n\n---\n\n**Drug-Gene Summary: Netilmicin + MT-RNR1 (Uncertain Risk)**\n\n**Implication**\nMT-RNR1 encodes the mitochondrial 12S ribosomal RNA. Well-characterized pathogenic variants (m.1555A>G, m.1494C>T) structurally mimic the bacterial ribosomal target, making the mitochondrial ribosomes in cochlear hair cells abnormally sensitive to aminoglycoside binding — resulting in irreversible sensorineural hearing loss even at standard doses. An *uncertain risk* variant may have a similar but unquantified effect on ribosomal structure and function.\n\n**Recommendation (Moderate)**\n- Prefer a non-aminoglycoside alternative when clinically viable.\n- If netilmicin is essential, use the minimum effective dose and duration.\n- Conduct baseline and serial audiological monitoring.\n- Provide informed consent regarding the potential (uncertain magnitude) risk of permanent hearing loss.\n\n**Why \"Moderate\" and not \"Strong\"?**\nA \"Strong\" avoidance recommendation (as per CPIC guidance) applies to *known* pathogenic MT-RNR1 variants. For uncertain-significance variants, the evidence does not yet support an absolute contraindication, but the irreversibility of ototoxicity justifies a precautionary moderate-strength recommendation to prefer alternatives."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":400,"cache_read_input_tokens":21188,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":400,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6fa84c80-c16b-44bb-8dbe-b015930c60ee","uuid":"10f3e7fc-48ab-48bd-8067-c591eafb55da"}

Well-characterized pathogenic variants (m.1555A>G, m.1494C>T) structurally mimic the bacterial ribosomal target, making the mitochondrial ribosomes in cochlear hair cells abnormally sensitive to aminoglycoside binding — resulting in irreversible sensorineural hearing loss even at standard doses. An *uncertain risk* variant may have a similar but unquantified effect on ribosomal structure and function.\n\n**Recommendation (Moderate)**\n- Prefer a non-aminoglycoside alternative when clinically viable.\n- If netilmicin is essential, use the minimum effective dose and duration.\n- Conduct baseline and serial audiological monitoring.\n- Provide informed consent regarding the potential (uncertain magnitude) risk of permanent hearing loss.\n\n**Why \"Moderate\" and not \"Strong\"?**\nA \"Strong\" avoidance recommendation (as per CPIC guidance) applies to *known* pathogenic MT-RNR1 variants.
